621: function buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken: string): Record<string, string> {
622:   const headers: Record<string, string> = {
623:     Accept: 'application/vnd.github+json',
624:     'X-GitHub-Api-Version': '2022-11-28',
625:     'User-Agent': 'nimbus-worker/1.0',
626:   };
627:   headers.Authorization = githubToken.toLowerCase().startsWith('bearer ') ? githubToken : `Bearer ${githubToken}`;
628:   return headers;
629: }
630: 
631: async function fetchGitHubJson(url: string, githubToken: string): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
632:   const response = await fetch(url, {
633:     headers: buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken),
634:   });
635: 
636:   if (!response.ok) {
637:     const rateLimited =
638:       response.status === 429 ||
639:       (response.status === 403 && (response.headers.get('x-ratelimit-remaining') ?? '').trim() === '0');
640:     const responseBody = redactReviewText(await response.text());
641:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
642:       'review_context_github_api_error',
643:       `GitHub API request failed (${response.status}) for ${url}${rateLimited ? ' [rate_limited]' : ''}`,
644:       responseBody
645:     );
646:   }
647: 
648:   const data = (await response.json()) as unknown;
649:   return asRecord(data);
650: }
651: 
652: async function fetchGitHubArray(url: string, githubToken: string): Promise<unknown[]> {
653:   const response = await fetch(url, {
654:     headers: buildGitHubHeaders(githubToken),
655:   });
656: 
657:   if (!response.ok) {
658:     const rateLimited =
659:       response.status === 429 ||
660:       (response.status === 403 && (response.headers.get('x-ratelimit-remaining') ?? '').trim() === '0');
661:     const responseBody = redactReviewText(await response.text());
662:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
663:       'review_context_github_api_error',
664:       `GitHub API request failed (${response.status}) for ${url}${rateLimited ? ' [rate_limited]' : ''}`,
665:       responseBody
666:     );
667:   }
668: 
669:   const data = (await response.json()) as unknown;
670:   return Array.isArray(data) ? data : [];
671: }
672: 
673: function classifyCochangeSkipReason(error: unknown): 'rate_limited' | 'github_api_error' | 'cache_error' {
674:   if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError && error.code === 'review_context_cache_error') {
675:     return 'cache_error';
676:   }
677:   if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError && /\[rate_limited\]/.test(error.message)) {
678:     return 'rate_limited';
679:   }
680:   if (isCochangeCacheError(error)) {
681:     return 'cache_error';
682:   }
683:   return 'github_api_error';
684: }
685: 
686: async function fetchCochangeFromCheckpointBranch(
687:   repo: string,
688:   changedPaths: string[],
689:   lookbackSessions: number,
690:   githubToken: string
691: ): Promise<{
692:   relatedByChangedPath: Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>;
693:   sessionsScanned: number;
694: }> {
695:   const commits = await fetchGitHubArray(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/commits?sha=entire/checkpoints/v1&per_page=${lookbackSessions}`, githubToken);
696: 
697:   const frequencyByChangedPath = new Map<string, Map<string, { count: number; sessions: Set<string> }>>();
698:   for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
699:     frequencyByChangedPath.set(changedPath, new Map());
700:   }
701:   let sessionsScanned = 0;
702: 
703:   for (const commit of commits.slice(0, lookbackSessions)) {
704:     const commitRecord = asRecord(commit);
705:     const sha = readOptionalString(commitRecord.sha);
706:     if (!sha) {
707:       continue;
708:     }
709: 
710:     const detail = await fetchGitHubJson(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/commits/${sha}`, githubToken);
711:     const files = Array.isArray(detail.files) ? detail.files : [];
712:     const metadataPaths = files
713:       .map((entry) => readOptionalString(asRecord(entry).filename))
714:       .filter((path): path is string => Boolean(path && path.endsWith('/metadata.json')))
715:       .slice(0, 3);
716: 
717:     const touchedFiles = new Set<string>();
718:     for (const metadataPath of metadataPaths) {
719:       const file = await fetchGitHubJson(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}/contents/${metadataPath}?ref=${sha}`, githubToken);
720:       const content = readOptionalString(file.content);
721:       if (!content) {
722:         continue;
723:       }
724:       let decoded = '';
725:       try {
726:         decoded = atob(content.replace(/\n/g, ''));
727:       } catch {
728:         continue;
729:       }
730:       try {
731:         const metadata = JSON.parse(decoded) as unknown;
732:         for (const path of parseTouchedFilesFromMetadata(asRecord(metadata))) {
733:           touchedFiles.add(path);
734:         }
735:       } catch {
736:         continue;
737:       }
738:     }
739: 
740:     if (touchedFiles.size === 0) {
741:       continue;
742:     }
743: 
744:     sessionsScanned += 1;
745:     for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
746:       if (!touchedFiles.has(changedPath)) {
747:         continue;
748:       }
749:       const frequency = frequencyByChangedPath.get(changedPath);
750:       if (!frequency) {
751:         continue;
752:       }
753:       for (const path of touchedFiles) {
754:         if (path === changedPath) {
755:           continue;
756:         }
757:         const next = frequency.get(path) ?? { count: 0, sessions: new Set<string>() };
758:         next.count += 1;
759:         next.sessions.add(sha);
760:         frequency.set(path, next);
761:       }
762:     }
763:   }
764: 
765:   const relatedByChangedPath: Record<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>> = {};
766:   for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
767:     const frequency =
768:       frequencyByChangedPath.get(changedPath) ?? new Map<string, { count: number; sessions: Set<string> }>();
769:     relatedByChangedPath[changedPath] = Array.from(frequency.entries())
770:       .map(([path, value]) => ({
771:         path,
772:         frequency: value.count,
773:         sessionIds: Array.from(value.sessions),
774:       }))
775:       .sort((left, right) => right.frequency - left.frequency);
776:   }
777: 
778:   return {
779:     relatedByChangedPath,
780:     sessionsScanned,
781:   };
782: }
783: 
784: async function assembleReviewContextBootstrap(
785:   env: Env,
786:   review: ReviewRunResponse,
787:   reviewPayload: Record<string, unknown>,
788:   options?: ReviewRunExecutionOptions
789: ): Promise<ReviewContext> {
790:   const COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS = 5;
791:   const COCHANGE_TOP_N = 20;
792:   const CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT = 10;
793: 
794:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
795:     reviewId: review.id,
796:     eventType: 'review_context_assembly_started',
797:     payload: {
798:       source: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
799:     },
800:   });
801: 
802:   const workspace = await getWorkspace(env.DB, review.workspaceId);
803:   const checkpointId = workspace?.checkpointId?.trim() ?? '';
804:   if (!checkpointId) {
805:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
806:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
807:       'Review context assembly requires an Entire checkpoint-backed workspace with a checkpointId.'
808:     );
809:   }
810: 
811:   const deployment = await getWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, review.workspaceId, review.deploymentId);
812:   if (!deployment) {
813:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError('review_context_deployment_not_found', `Deployment ${review.deploymentId} was not found.`);
814:   }
815: 
816:   const deploymentRequest = (await getWorkspaceDeploymentRequestPayload(env.DB, review.deploymentId)) ?? {};
817:   const deploymentRequestProvenance = asRecord(deploymentRequest.provenance);
818:   const reviewRequestProvenance = asRecord(reviewPayload.provenance);
819:   const requestProvenance = mergeProvenance(deploymentRequestProvenance, reviewRequestProvenance);
820:   const sessionIds = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(requestProvenance.sessionIds));
821:   if (sessionIds.length === 0) {
822:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
823:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
824:       'Review context assembly requires at least one Entire sessionId in deployment provenance.'
825:     );
826:   }
827: 
828:   const sessionId = sessionIds[0] ?? '';
829:   const sessionIntentCandidates = uniqueStrings(parseStringArray(requestProvenance.intentSessionContext));
830:   const sessionIntent = sessionIntentCandidates[0] ?? null;
831:   const attributionTrailer = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.attributionTrailer);
832:   const agentType = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.agentType);
833:   const requestedTokenBudget =
834:     readOptionalNumber(requestProvenance.reviewContextTokenBudget) ??
835:     readOptionalNumber(requestProvenance.contextTokenBudget) ??
836:     null;
837:   const configuredTokenBudget = readOptionalNumber(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET);
838:   const tokenBudget = requestedTokenBudget ?? configuredTokenBudget ?? null;
839: 
840:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
841:     reviewId: review.id,
842:     eventType: 'review_context_checkpoint_context_collected',
843:     payload: {
844:       checkpointId,
845:       sessionId,
846:       sessionCount: sessionIds.length,
847:       hasSessionIntent: Boolean(sessionIntent),
848:     },
849:   });
850: 
851:   const result = asRecord(deployment.result);
852:   const resultProvenance = asRecord(result.provenance);
853:   const resultArtifact = asRecord(result.artifact);
854:   const provenanceOperationId = typeof resultProvenance.operationId === 'string'
855:     ? resultProvenance.operationId
856:     : typeof requestProvenance.operationId === 'string'
857:       ? requestProvenance.operationId
858:       : null;
859:   const reviewDiffArtifactId = typeof resultArtifact.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
860:     ? resultArtifact.reviewDiffArtifactId
861:     : typeof resultProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
862:       ? resultProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId
863:       : typeof requestProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId === 'string'
864:         ? requestProvenance.reviewDiffArtifactId
865:         : null;
866: 
867:   const authoritativeDiff = await loadAuthoritativeDeploymentDiff(
868:     env,
869:     review.workspaceId,
870:     provenanceOperationId,
871:     reviewDiffArtifactId
872:   );
873:   const commitDiffPatch = readOptionalString(requestProvenance.commitDiffPatch);
874:   const authoritativeDiffPatch = readOptionalString(authoritativeDiff?.patch);
875:   const diffPatch = authoritativeDiffPatch ?? commitDiffPatch ?? null;
876:   if (!diffPatch) {
877:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
878:       'review_context_diff_missing',
879:       'Review context assembly requires non-empty diff patch context. Ensure deployment provenance includes review diff artifact or commit diff patch.'
880:     );
881:   }
882:   const changedPaths = diffPatch ? parseChangedPathsFromDiff(diffPatch) : [];
883:   const diffHunks = diffPatch ? parseDiffHunks(diffPatch) : [];
884: 
885:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
886:     reviewId: review.id,
887:     eventType: 'review_context_diff_collected',
888:     payload: {
889:       source: authoritativeDiffPatch ? authoritativeDiff?.source ?? null : commitDiffPatch ? 'commit_patch' : null,
890:       artifactId: authoritativeDiffPatch ? authoritativeDiff?.artifactId ?? null : null,
891:       hasDiff: Boolean(diffPatch),
892:       patchBytes: diffPatch ? new TextEncoder().encode(diffPatch).byteLength : 0,
893:       fallbackUsed: !authoritativeDiffPatch && Boolean(commitDiffPatch),
894:     },
895:   });
896: 
897:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
898:     reviewId: review.id,
899:     eventType: 'review_context_changed_files_collected',
900:     payload: {
901:       changedFileCount: changedPaths.length,
902:     },
903:   });
904: 
905:   const deploymentSourceBundleKey =
906:     typeof resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey === 'string' && resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey.trim()
907:       ? resultArtifact.sourceBundleKey.trim()
908:       : deployment.sourceBundleKey ?? null;
909:   if (!deploymentSourceBundleKey) {
910:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
911:       'review_context_source_bundle_missing',
912:       'Review context assembly requires deployment source bundle key.'
913:     );
914:   }
915: 
916:   const changedFileReads = changedPaths.length
917:     ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
918:         sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
919:         sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-changed`,
920:         paths: changedPaths,
921:       })
922:     : [];
923:   const changedFiles = changedFileReads
924:     .filter((item) => item.content !== null && !item.error)
925:     .map((item) => ({
926:       path: item.path,
927:       content: item.content ?? '',
928:       byteSize: item.bytes,
929:       source: 'changed' as const,
930:     }));
931: 
932:   const includeConventions = parseBoolean(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_INCLUDE_CONVENTIONS, false);
933:   const conventionCandidates = includeConventions ? discoverConventionCandidates(changedPaths, CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT) : [];
934:   const conventionReads = includeConventions && conventionCandidates.length
935:     ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
936:         sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
937:         sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-conventions`,
938:         paths: conventionCandidates,
939:       })
940:     : [];
941:   const conventionFiles = conventionReads
942:     .filter((item) => item.content !== null && !item.error)
943:     .slice(0, CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT)
944:     .map((item) => ({
945:       path: item.path,
946:       content: item.content ?? '',
947:       byteSize: item.bytes,
948:       source: 'convention' as const,
949:     }));
950: 
951:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
952:     reviewId: review.id,
953:     eventType: 'review_context_conventions_collected',
954:     payload: {
955:       candidateCount: conventionCandidates.length,
956:       conventionFileCount: conventionFiles.length,
957:       maxCount: CONVENTION_FILE_MAX_COUNT,
958:       skipped: !includeConventions,
959:     },
960:   });
961: 
962:   const repoSlug =
963:     readOptionalString(requestProvenance.repo) ??
964:     readOptionalString(requestProvenance.repository) ??
965:     readOptionalString(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_REPO);
966:   if (!repoSlug) {
967:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
968:       'unsupported_without_entire_checkpoint_context',
969:       'Review context assembly requires repository slug in deployment provenance (provenance.repo).'
970:     );
971:   }
972:   let relatedFiles: Array<{
973:     path: string;
974:     content: string;
975:     byteSize: number;
976:     source: 'related';
977:     score: number;
978:     coChangeFrequency: number;
979:     supportingSessionIds: string[];
980:   }> = [];
981:   let sessionsScanned = 0;
982:   let coChangeSkipped = false;
983:   let coChangeSkipReason: string | null = null;
984:   let coChangeAvailable = false;
985:   const githubToken = readOptionalString(options?.cochangeGithubToken) ?? readOptionalString(env.REVIEW_CONTEXT_GITHUB_TOKEN);
986: 
987:   if (!githubToken) {
988:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
989:       'review_context_github_token_missing',
990:       'co-change retrieval requires a GitHub token - set REVIEW_CONTEXT_GITHUB_TOKEN in your local .env'
991:     );
992:   } else {
993:     try {
994:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
995:         reviewId: review.id,
996:         eventType: 'review_context_cochange_lookup_started',
997:         payload: {
998:           repo: repoSlug,
999:           lookbackSessions: COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1000:         },
1001:       });
1002: 
1003:       const relatedFrequency = new Map<string, { frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>();
1004:       const entriesByChangedPath = new Map<string, Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>>();
1005:       const changedPathsMissingCache: string[] = [];
1006:       const cachedRows = await getReviewCochangeCacheBatch(env.DB, {
1007:         repo: repoSlug,
1008:         filePaths: changedPaths,
1009:       });
1010:       const cacheByPath = new Map(cachedRows.map((row) => [row.filePath, row]));
1011: 
1012:       for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
1013:         const cached = cacheByPath.get(changedPath);
1014:         const entries = cached?.cochange;
1015:         const cachedLookbackSessions = cached?.lookbackSessions ?? null;
1016:         if (entries && cachedLookbackSessions === COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS) {
1017:           entriesByChangedPath.set(changedPath, entries);
1018:         } else {
1019:           changedPathsMissingCache.push(changedPath);
1020:         }
1021:       }
1022: 
1023:       if (changedPathsMissingCache.length > 0) {
1024:         const fetched = await fetchCochangeFromCheckpointBranch(repoSlug, changedPathsMissingCache, COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS, githubToken);
1025:         sessionsScanned += fetched.sessionsScanned;
1026:         const cacheUpserts: Array<{
1027:           filePath: string;
1028:           repo: string;
1029:           branch: string;
1030:           cochange: Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>;
1031:           lookbackSessions: number;
1032:         }> = [];
1033:         for (const changedPath of changedPathsMissingCache) {
1034:           const entries = fetched.relatedByChangedPath[changedPath] ?? [];
1035:           entriesByChangedPath.set(changedPath, entries);
1036:           cacheUpserts.push({
1037:             filePath: changedPath,
1038:             repo: repoSlug,
1039:             branch: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
1040:             cochange: entries,
1041:             lookbackSessions: COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1042:           });
1043:         }
1044:         await upsertReviewCochangeCacheBatch(env.DB, cacheUpserts);
1045:       }
1046: 
1047:       for (const changedPath of changedPaths) {
1048:         const entries = entriesByChangedPath.get(changedPath) ?? [];
1049:         for (const entry of entries) {
1050:           const existing = relatedFrequency.get(entry.path) ?? { frequency: 0, sessionIds: [] };
1051:           existing.frequency += entry.frequency;
1052:           existing.sessionIds = Array.from(new Set([...existing.sessionIds, ...entry.sessionIds]));
1053:           relatedFrequency.set(entry.path, existing);
1054:         }
1055:       }
1056: 
1057:       const changedPathSet = new Set(changedPaths);
1058:       const rankedRelated = Array.from(relatedFrequency.entries())
1059:         .map(([path, value]) => ({ path, ...value }))
1060:         .filter((item) => !changedPathSet.has(item.path))
1061:         .sort((left, right) => right.frequency - left.frequency)
1062:         .slice(0, COCHANGE_TOP_N);
1063: 
1064:       const relatedReads = rankedRelated.length
1065:         ? await readWorkspaceFilesFromSourceBundle(env, {
1066:             sourceBundleKey: deploymentSourceBundleKey,
1067:             sandboxId: `review-context-${review.id}-related`,
1068:             paths: rankedRelated.map((item) => item.path),
1069:           })
1070:         : [];
1071:       const readByPath = new Map(relatedReads.map((item) => [item.path, item]));
1072:       relatedFiles = rankedRelated
1073:         .flatMap((item) => {
1074:           const read = readByPath.get(item.path);
1075:           if (!read || read.content === null || read.error) {
1076:             return [];
1077:           }
1078:           return [
1079:             {
1080:               path: item.path,
1081:               content: read.content,
1082:               byteSize: read.bytes,
1083:               source: 'related' as const,
1084:               score: item.frequency,
1085:               coChangeFrequency: item.frequency,
1086:               supportingSessionIds: item.sessionIds,
1087:             },
1088:           ];
1089:         });
1090:       coChangeAvailable = relatedFiles.length > 0;
1091: 
1092:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1093:         reviewId: review.id,
1094:         eventType: 'review_context_cochange_lookup_completed',
1095:         payload: {
1096:           repo: repoSlug,
1097:           relatedFileCount: relatedFiles.length,
1098:           topN: COCHANGE_TOP_N,
1099:         },
1100:       });
1101:     } catch (error) {
1102:       const reason = classifyCochangeSkipReason(error);
1103:       const sanitizedErrorDetails = redactReviewText(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
1104:       const cacheErrorDetails = isCochangeCacheError(error) ? sanitizedErrorDetails : null;
1105:       await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1106:         reviewId: review.id,
1107:         eventType: 'review_context_cochange_failed',
1108:         payload: {
1109:           reason,
1110:           repo: repoSlug,
1111:           lookbackSessions: COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1112:           githubResponseBody: error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError ? error.details : sanitizedErrorDetails,
1113:         },
1114:       });
1115:       if (error instanceof ReviewContextAssemblyError) {
1116:         throw error;
1117:       }
1118:       if (reason === 'cache_error') {
1119:         throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1120:           'review_context_cache_error',
1121:           'Co-change context cache read/write failed (cache_error).',
1122:           cacheErrorDetails
1123:         );
1124:       }
1125:       throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1126:         'review_context_github_api_error',
1127:         `Co-change context retrieval failed (${reason}).`
1128:       );
1129:     }
1130:   }
1131: 
1132:   const assembledAt = new Date().toISOString();
1133:   const contextId = generateReviewContextId();
1134:   const contextPayload: ReviewContext = {
1135:     id: contextId,
1136:     reviewId: review.id,
1137:     workspaceId: review.workspaceId,
1138:     deploymentId: review.deploymentId,
1139:     commitSha: workspace?.commitSha ?? '',
1140:     assembledAt,
1141:     checkpoint: {
1142:       checkpointId,
1143:       branch: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
1144:       attributionTrailer,
1145:       session: {
1146:         sessionId,
1147:         agentType,
1148:         sessionIntent,
1149:       },
1150:     },
1151:     retrieval: {
1152:       changedFiles,
1153:       diffHunks,
1154:       relatedFiles,
1155:       conventionFiles,
1156:       coChange: {
1157:         source: 'entire/checkpoints/v1',
1158:         lookbackSessions: COCHANGE_LOOKBACK_SESSIONS,
1159:         sessionsScanned,
1160:         filesConsidered: changedPaths.length,
1161:         topN: COCHANGE_TOP_N,
1162:         coChangeSkipped,
1163:         coChangeSkipReason,
1164:         coChangeAvailable,
1165:       },
1166:     },
1167:     stats: {
1168:       totalFilesIncluded: changedFiles.length + relatedFiles.length + conventionFiles.length,
1169:       totalBytesIncluded:
1170:         changedFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0) +
1171:         relatedFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0) +
1172:         conventionFiles.reduce((total, item) => total + item.byteSize, 0),
1173:       estimatedTokens: estimateTokenCount([
1174:         diffPatch ?? '',
1175:         ...changedFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1176:         ...relatedFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1177:         ...conventionFiles.map((item) => item.content),
1178:       ]),
1179:       tokenBudget,
1180:     },
1181:   };
1182: 
1183:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1184:     reviewId: review.id,
1185:     eventType: 'review_context_budget_checked',
1186:     payload: {
1187:       estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1188:       tokenBudget,
1189:       tokenBudgetSource:
1190:         requestedTokenBudget !== null
1191:           ? 'request_provenance'
1192:           : configuredTokenBudget !== null
1193:             ? 'configured_env'
1194:             : 'unconfigured',
1195:       exceeded: tokenBudget !== null && contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens > tokenBudget,
1196:     },
1197:   });
1198: 
1199:   if (tokenBudget !== null && contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens > tokenBudget) {
1200:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1201:       'review_context_budget_exceeded',
1202:       `ReviewContext estimated token usage (${contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens}) exceeds configured budget (${tokenBudget}). Increase the budget and retry.`
1203:     );
1204:   }
1205: 
1206:   const storageBucketCandidates = [env.REVIEW_CONTEXTS, env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS, env.SOURCE_BUNDLES];
1207:   const storageBucket = storageBucketCandidates.find(
1208:     (bucket): bucket is R2Bucket => Boolean(bucket && typeof bucket.put === 'function')
1209:   );
1210:   if (!storageBucket) {
1211:     throw new ReviewContextAssemblyError(
1212:       'review_context_storage_unavailable',
1213:       'REVIEW_CONTEXTS, WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS, or SOURCE_BUNDLES R2 binding is required for review context assembly.'
1214:     );
1215:   }
1216: 
1217:   const r2Key = `review-context/${review.id}/${contextId}.json`;
1218:   const serialized = JSON.stringify(stripSensitiveTokenFields(contextPayload));
1219:   await storageBucket.put(r2Key, serialized, {
1220:     httpMetadata: {
1221:       contentType: 'application/json',
1222:     },
1223:   });
1224:   const ref = await createReviewContextBlobReference(env.DB, {
1225:     id: contextId,
1226:     reviewId: review.id,
1227:     workspaceId: review.workspaceId,
1228:     deploymentId: review.deploymentId,
1229:     r2Key,
1230:     byteSize: new TextEncoder().encode(serialized).byteLength,
1231:     estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1232:   });
1233: 
1234:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1235:     reviewId: review.id,
1236:     eventType: 'review_context_stored',
1237:     payload: {
1238:       contextId: ref.id,
1239:       r2Key: ref.r2Key,
1240:       totalFilesIncluded: contextPayload.stats.totalFilesIncluded,
1241:       totalBytesIncluded: contextPayload.stats.totalBytesIncluded,
1242:       estimatedTokens: contextPayload.stats.estimatedTokens,
1243:     },
1244:   });
1245: 
1246:   await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {
1247:     reviewId: review.id,
1248:     eventType: 'review_context_assembly_succeeded',
1249:     payload: {
1250:       checkpointId,
1251:       sessionId,
1252:       changedFileCount: changedPaths.length,
1253:       contextId: ref.id,
1254:     },
1255:   });
1256: 
1257:   return contextPayload;
1258: }
1259: 
1260: async function loadAuthoritativeDeploymentDiff(
1261:   env: Env,
1262:   workspaceId: string,
1263:   operationId: string | null,
1264:   reviewDiffArtifactId: string | null
1265: ): Promise<{ source: 'artifact_patch'; artifactId: string; patch: string } | null> {
1266:   const getArtifactObject = async (objectKey: string): Promise<R2ObjectBody | null> => {
1267:     const fromArtifacts = env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS ? await env.WORKSPACE_ARTIFACTS.get(objectKey) : null;
1268:     if (fromArtifacts) {
1269:       return fromArtifacts;
1270:     }
1271:     return env.SOURCE_BUNDLES ? await env.SOURCE_BUNDLES.get(objectKey) : null;
1272:   };
1273: 
1274:   if (reviewDiffArtifactId) {
1275:     const reviewArtifact = await getWorkspaceArtifactById(env.DB, workspaceId, reviewDiffArtifactId);
1276:     if (reviewArtifact && reviewArtifact.artifact.type === 'patch') {
1277:       const object = await getArtifactObject(reviewArtifact.objectKey);
1278:       if (!object) {
1279:         return null;
1280:       }
1281:       return {
1282:         source: 'artifact_patch',
1283:         artifactId: reviewDiffArtifactId,
1284:         patch: await object.text(),
1285:       };
1286:     }
1287:   }
1288: 
1289:   if (!operationId) {
1290:     return null;
1291:   }
1292: 
1293:   const operation = await getWorkspaceOperation(env.DB, workspaceId, operationId);
1294:   if (!operation || operation.type !== 'export_patch' || operation.status !== 'succeeded') {
1295:     return null;
1296:   }
1297: 
1298:   const result = asRecord(operation.result);
1299:   const artifactId = typeof result.artifactId === 'string' ? result.artifactId.trim() : '';
1300:   if (!artifactId) {
1301:     return null;
1302:   }
1303: 
1304:   const artifact = await getWorkspaceArtifactById(env.DB, workspaceId, artifactId);
1305:   if (!artifact || artifact.artifact.type !== 'patch') {
1306:     return null;
1307:   }
1308: 
1309:   const object = await getArtifactObject(artifact.objectKey);
1310:   if (!object) {
1311:     return null;
1312:   }
1313: 
1314:   return {
1315:     source: 'artifact_patch',
1316:     artifactId,
1317:     patch: await object.text(),
1318:   };
1319: }
1320: 
1321: async function buildWorkspaceDeploymentReport(
1322:   env: Env,
1323:   review: ReviewRunResponse,
1324:   payload: Record<string, unknown>,
1325:   reviewContext: ReviewContext
1326: ): Promise<ReviewReport> {
1327:   const deployment = await getWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, review.workspaceId, review.deploymentId);
1328:   if (!deployment) {
1329:     throw new Error(`Deployment not found for review target ${review.deploymentId}`);

484: function stripSensitiveTokenFields(value: unknown): unknown {
485:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
486:     return value.map((item) => stripSensitiveTokenFields(item));
487:   }
488:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
489:     if (typeof value === 'string' && GITHUB_TOKEN_PATTERN_TEST.test(value)) {

494:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
495:   return Object.entries(record).reduce<Record<string, unknown>>((result, [key, nested]) => {

497:     if (
498:       normalizedKey === 'x-review-github-token' ||
499:       normalizedKey === 'review_context_github_token' ||
500:       normalizedKey === 'authorization'

641: function toReviewFindingRecord(value: unknown): ReviewFinding[] {
642:   if (!Array.isArray(value)) {
643:     return [];
644:   }
645: 
646:   // Phase 2 contract: strict V2 query surfaces intentionally exclude legacy finding shapes.
647:   // We do not attempt semantic V1->V2 conversion at read time because legacy reports were
648:   // generated without full ReviewContext and are not trustworthy for canonical V2 semantics.
649:   return value.flatMap((item) => {
650:     const record = item && typeof item === 'object' && !Array.isArray(item) ? (item as Record<string, unknown>) : null;
651:     if (!record) {
652:       return [];
653:     }
654: 
655:     const severity = typeof record.severity === 'string' ? record.severity.trim() : '';
656:     const category = typeof record.category === 'string' ? record.category.trim() : '';
657:     const passType = typeof record.passType === 'string' ? record.passType.trim() : '';
658:     const description = typeof record.description === 'string' ? record.description.trim() : '';
659:     const suggestedFix = typeof record.suggestedFix === 'string' ? record.suggestedFix.trim() : '';
660:     if (!severity || !category || !passType || !description) {
661:       return [];
662:     }
663: 
664:     if (!['info', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'].includes(severity)) {
665:       return [];
666:     }
667:     if (!['security', 'logic', 'style', 'breaking-change'].includes(category)) {
668:       return [];
669:     }
670:     if (passType !== 'single') {
671:       return [];
672:     }
673: 
674:     const locationsRaw = Array.isArray(record.locations) ? record.locations : [];
675:     const locations = locationsRaw.flatMap((locationItem) => {
676:       const location =
677:         locationItem && typeof locationItem === 'object' && !Array.isArray(locationItem)
678:           ? (locationItem as Record<string, unknown>)
679:           : null;
680:       if (!location) {
681:         return [];
682:       }
683: 
684:       const filePath = typeof location.filePath === 'string' ? location.filePath.trim() : '';
685:       if (!filePath) {
686:         return [];
687:       }
688:       const startLine = location.startLine;
689:       const endLine = location.endLine;
690:       const nullRange = startLine === null && endLine === null;
691:       const numericRange =
692:         typeof startLine === 'number' &&
693:         Number.isInteger(startLine) &&
694:         startLine > 0 &&
695:         typeof endLine === 'number' &&
696:         Number.isInteger(endLine) &&
697:         endLine >= startLine;
698:       if (!nullRange && !numericRange) {
699:         return [];
700:       }
701: 
702:       return [
703:         {
704:           filePath,
705:           startLine: nullRange ? null : (startLine as number),
706:           endLine: nullRange ? null : (endLine as number),
707:         },
708:       ];
709:     });
710: 
711:     if (locations.length === 0) {
712:       return [];
713:     }
714: 
715:     return [
716:       {
717:         severity: severity as ReviewFinding['severity'],
718:         category: category as ReviewFinding['category'],
719:         passType: passType as ReviewFinding['passType'],
720:         locations,
721:         description,
722:         suggestedFix,
723:       },
724:     ];
725:   });
726: }

728: function toReviewRunResponse(record: ReviewRunRecord): ReviewRunResponse {
729:   const provenance = parseJsonOrFallback(record.provenance_json, {});
730:   const report = parseJsonOrFallback(record.report_json, null) as ReviewReport | null;
731:   const reportHasProvenance = Boolean(report?.provenance);
732: 
733:   const response: ReviewRunResponse = {
734:     id: record.id,
735:     workspaceId: record.workspace_id,
736:     deploymentId: record.deployment_id,
737:     target: {
738:       type: record.target_type,
739:       workspaceId: record.workspace_id,
740:       deploymentId: record.deployment_id,
741:     },
742:     mode: record.mode,
743:     status: record.status,
744:     idempotencyKey: record.idempotency_key,
745:     attemptCount: record.attempt_count,
746:     startedAt: record.started_at,
747:     finishedAt: record.finished_at,
748:     createdAt: record.created_at,
749:     updatedAt: record.updated_at,
750:     findings: toReviewFindingRecord(report?.findings),
751:     evidence: Array.isArray(report?.evidence) ? report.evidence : [],
752:     provenance: {
753:       sessionIds:
754:         report?.provenance && Array.isArray(report.provenance.sessionIds) ? report.provenance.sessionIds : [],
755:       promptSummary:
756:         report?.provenance && typeof report.provenance.promptSummary === 'string'
757:           ? report.provenance.promptSummary
758:           : !reportHasProvenance && typeof (provenance as Record<string, unknown>).promptSummary === 'string'
759:             ? ((provenance as Record<string, unknown>).promptSummary as string)
760:             : null,
761:       transcriptUrl:
762:         report?.provenance && typeof report.provenance.transcriptUrl === 'string'
763:           ? report.provenance.transcriptUrl
764:           : null,
765:       reviewContextRef:
766:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.reviewContextRef && typeof report.provenance.reviewContextRef === 'object'
767:           ? (report.provenance.reviewContextRef as ReviewContextRef)
768:           : null,
769:       reviewContextStats:
770:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.reviewContextStats && typeof report.provenance.reviewContextStats === 'object'
771:           ? report.provenance.reviewContextStats
772:           : undefined,
773:       coChange:
774:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.coChange && typeof report.provenance.coChange === 'object'
775:           ? report.provenance.coChange
776:           : undefined,
777:       contextResolution:
778:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.contextResolution && typeof report.provenance.contextResolution === 'object'
779:           ? report.provenance.contextResolution
780:           : undefined,
781:       outputSchemaVersion: report?.provenance?.outputSchemaVersion,
782:       passArchitecture: report?.provenance?.passArchitecture,
783:       validation:
784:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.validation && typeof report.provenance.validation === 'object'
785:           ? report.provenance.validation
786:           : undefined,
787:       furtherPassesLowYield:
788:         report?.provenance && report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield && typeof report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield === 'object'
789:           ? report.provenance.furtherPassesLowYield
790:           : undefined,
791:       advisories:
792:         report?.provenance && Array.isArray(report.provenance.advisories)
793:           ? report.provenance.advisories.filter((item): item is string => typeof item === 'string')
794:           : undefined,
795:     },
796:     markdownSummary: record.markdown_summary,
797:   };
798: 
799:   if (report?.summary) {
800:     response.summary = report.summary;
801:   }
802:   if (typeof report?.summaryText === 'string') {
803:     response.summaryText = report.summaryText;
804:   }
805:   if (typeof report?.furtherPassesLowYield === 'boolean') {
806:     response.furtherPassesLowYield = report.furtherPassesLowYield;
807:   }
808:   if (report?.intent) {
809:     response.intent = report.intent;
810:   }
811:   if (record.error_code && record.error_message) {
812:     response.error = {
813:       code: record.error_code,
814:       message: record.error_message,
815:     };
816:   }
817: 
818:   return response;
819: }

821: /**
822:  * Create a new checkpoint job in the database
823:  */
824: export async function createCheckpointJob(
825:   db: D1Database,
826:   input: CreateCheckpointJobInput
827: ): Promise<JobResponse> {
828:   const result = await db
829:     .prepare(
830:       `INSERT INTO jobs (
831:          id,
832:          prompt,
833:          model,
834:          status,
835:          phase,
836:          source_type,
837:          checkpoint_id,
838:          commit_sha,
839:          source_ref,
840:          source_project_root,
841:          build_run_tests_if_present,
842:          build_run_lint_if_present,
843:          source_bundle_key,
844:          source_bundle_sha256,
845:          source_bundle_bytes
846:        )
847:        VALUES (?, ?, 'checkpoint', 'queued', 'queued', 'checkpoint', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
848:        RETURNING *`
849:     )
850:     .bind(
851:       input.id,
852:       input.prompt,
853:       input.checkpointId,
854:       input.commitSha,
855:       input.sourceRef ?? null,
856:       input.sourceProjectRoot ?? null,
857:       input.buildRunTestsIfPresent ? 1 : 0,
858:       input.buildRunLintIfPresent ? 1 : 0,
859:       input.sourceBundleKey,
860:       input.sourceBundleSha256,
861:       input.sourceBundleBytes
862:     )
863:     .first<JobRecord>();
864: 
865:   if (!result) {
866:     throw new Error('Failed to create checkpoint job');
867:   }
868: 
869:   return toJobResponse(result);
870: }

907: /**
908:  * Atomically claim a queued checkpoint job for execution.
909:  * Returns true when claim succeeds, false when another worker already claimed.
910:  */
911: export async function claimQueuedCheckpointJob(db: D1Database, id: string): Promise<boolean> {
912:   const result = await db
913:     .prepare(
914:       `UPDATE jobs
915:        SET status = 'running',
916:            phase = 'building',
917:            started_at = ?,
918:            error_message = NULL,
919:            error_code = NULL
920:        WHERE id = ? AND status = 'queued'`
921:     )
922:     .bind(new Date().toISOString(), id)
923:     .run();
924: 
925:   const changes = Number((result as { meta?: { changes?: number } }).meta?.changes ?? 0);
926:   return changes > 0;
927: }

929: /**
930:  * Append a sequenced job event and return its sequence number.
931:  */
932: export async function appendJobEvent(
933:   db: D1Database,
934:   input: {
935:     jobId: string;
936:     attemptNo?: number;
937:     eventType: string;
938:     phase: JobPhase;
939:     payload: unknown;
940:   }
941: ): Promise<number> {
942:   const sequenceResult = await db
943:     .prepare('UPDATE jobs SET last_event_seq = last_event_seq + 1 WHERE id = ? RETURNING last_event_seq')
944:     .bind(input.jobId)
945:     .first<{ last_event_seq: number }>();
946: 
947:   if (!sequenceResult) {
948:     throw new Error(`Failed to allocate event sequence for job ${input.jobId}`);
949:   }
950: 
951:   const seq = Number(sequenceResult.last_event_seq);
952:   await db
953:     .prepare(
954:       `INSERT INTO job_events (job_id, attempt_no, seq, event_type, phase, payload_json)
955:        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
956:     )
957:     .bind(
958:       input.jobId,
959:       input.attemptNo ?? 0,
960:       seq,
961:       input.eventType,
962:       input.phase,
963:       JSON.stringify(input.payload)
964:     )
965:     .run();
966: 
967:   return seq;
968: }

970: /**
971:  * List persisted job events from a given sequence.
972:  */
973: export async function listJobEvents(
974:   db: D1Database,
975:   jobId: string,
976:   fromExclusive = 0,
977:   limit = 500
978: ): Promise<JobEventItem[]> {
979:   const result = await db
980:     .prepare(
981:       `SELECT id, job_id, attempt_no, seq, event_type, phase, payload_json, created_at
982:        FROM job_events
983:        WHERE job_id = ? AND seq > ?
984:        ORDER BY seq ASC
985:        LIMIT ?`
986:     )
987:     .bind(jobId, fromExclusive, limit)
988:     .all<JobEventRecord>();
989: 
990:   return result.results.map((row) => {
991:     let payload: unknown;
992:     try {
993:       payload = JSON.parse(row.payload_json);
994:     } catch {
995:       payload = { raw: row.payload_json };
996:     }
997: 
998:     return {
999:       seq: row.seq,
1000:       eventType: row.event_type,
1001:       phase: row.phase,
1002:       payload,
1003:       createdAt: row.created_at,
1004:     };
1005:   });
1006: }

1008: /**
1009:  * Update job status
1010:  */
1011: export async function updateJobStatus(
1012:   db: D1Database,
1013:   id: string,
1014:   status: JobStatus,
1015:   additionalFields?: {
1016:     phase?: JobPhase;
1017:     started_at?: string | null;
1018:     completed_at?: string | null;
1019:     cancel_requested_at?: string | null;
1020:     cancelled_at?: string | null;
1021:     preview_url?: string;
1022:     deployed_url?: string;
1023:     code_url?: string;
1024:     code_zip_url?: string;
1025:     error_message?: string | null;
1026:     error_code?: string | null;
1027:     retry_count?: number;
1028:     file_count?: number;
1029:     // Metrics fields
1030:     prompt_tokens?: number;
1031:     completion_tokens?: number;
1032:     total_tokens?: number;
1033:     cost?: number;
1034:     llm_latency_ms?: number;
1035:     install_duration_ms?: number;
1036:     build_duration_ms?: number;
1037:     deploy_duration_ms?: number;
1038:     total_duration_ms?: number;
1039:     lines_of_code?: number;
1040:   }
1041: ): Promise<void> {
1042:   const updates: string[] = ['status = ?'];
1043:   const values: (string | number | null)[] = [status];
1044: 
1045:   if (additionalFields) {
1046:     if (additionalFields.phase !== undefined) {
1047:       updates.push('phase = ?');
1048:       values.push(additionalFields.phase);
1049:     }
1050:     if (additionalFields.started_at !== undefined) {
1051:       updates.push('started_at = ?');
1052:       values.push(additionalFields.started_at);
1053:     }
1054:     if (additionalFields.completed_at !== undefined) {
1055:       updates.push('completed_at = ?');
1056:       values.push(additionalFields.completed_at);
1057:     }
1058:     if (additionalFields.cancel_requested_at !== undefined) {
1059:       updates.push('cancel_requested_at = ?');
1060:       values.push(additionalFields.cancel_requested_at);
1061:     }
1062:     if (additionalFields.cancelled_at !== undefined) {
1063:       updates.push('cancelled_at = ?');
1064:       values.push(additionalFields.cancelled_at);
1065:     }
1066:     if (additionalFields.preview_url !== undefined) {
1067:       updates.push('preview_url = ?');
1068:       values.push(additionalFields.preview_url);
1069:     }
1070:     if (additionalFields.deployed_url !== undefined) {
1071:       updates.push('deployed_url = ?');
1072:       values.push(additionalFields.deployed_url);
1073:     }
1074:     if (additionalFields.code_url !== undefined) {
1075:       updates.push('code_url = ?');
1076:       values.push(additionalFields.code_url);
1077:     }
1078:     if (additionalFields.code_zip_url !== undefined) {
1079:       updates.push('code_zip_url = ?');
1080:       values.push(additionalFields.code_zip_url);
1081:     }
1082:     if (additionalFields.error_message !== undefined) {
1083:       updates.push('error_message = ?');
1084:       values.push(additionalFields.error_message);
1085:     }
1086:     if (additionalFields.error_code !== undefined) {
1087:       updates.push('error_code = ?');
1088:       values.push(additionalFields.error_code);
1089:     }
1090:     if (additionalFields.retry_count !== undefined) {
1091:       updates.push('retry_count = ?');
1092:       values.push(additionalFields.retry_count);
1093:     }
1094:     if (additionalFields.file_count !== undefined) {
1095:       updates.push('file_count = ?');
1096:       values.push(additionalFields.file_count);
1097:     }
1098:     // Metrics fields
1099:     if (additionalFields.prompt_tokens !== undefined) {
1100:       updates.push('prompt_tokens = ?');
1101:       values.push(additionalFields.prompt_tokens);
1102:     }
1103:     if (additionalFields.completion_tokens !== undefined) {
1104:       updates.push('completion_tokens = ?');
1105:       values.push(additionalFields.completion_tokens);
1106:     }
1107:     if (additionalFields.total_tokens !== undefined) {
1108:       updates.push('total_tokens = ?');
1109:       values.push(additionalFields.total_tokens);
1110:     }
1111:     if (additionalFields.cost !== undefined) {
1112:       updates.push('cost = ?');
1113:       values.push(additionalFields.cost);
1114:     }
1115:     if (additionalFields.llm_latency_ms !== undefined) {
1116:       updates.push('llm_latency_ms = ?');
1117:       values.push(additionalFields.llm_latency_ms);
1118:     }
1119:     if (additionalFields.install_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1120:       updates.push('install_duration_ms = ?');
1121:       values.push(additionalFields.install_duration_ms);
1122:     }
1123:     if (additionalFields.build_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1124:       updates.push('build_duration_ms = ?');
1125:       values.push(additionalFields.build_duration_ms);
1126:     }
1127:     if (additionalFields.deploy_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1128:       updates.push('deploy_duration_ms = ?');
1129:       values.push(additionalFields.deploy_duration_ms);
1130:     }
1131:     if (additionalFields.total_duration_ms !== undefined) {
1132:       updates.push('total_duration_ms = ?');
1133:       values.push(additionalFields.total_duration_ms);
1134:     }
1135:     if (additionalFields.lines_of_code !== undefined) {
1136:       updates.push('lines_of_code = ?');
1137:       values.push(additionalFields.lines_of_code);
1138:     }
1139:   }
1140: 
1141:   values.push(id);
1142: 
1143:   await db
1144:     .prepare(`UPDATE jobs SET ${updates.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?`)
1145:     .bind(...values)
1146:     .run();
1147: }

1234:   },

1237:   }

1239:   const updates: string[] = ['status = ?', 'updated_at = ?'];

1241:

1243:     if (additionalFields.baseline_ready !== undefined) {
1244:       updates.push('baseline_ready = ?');
1245:       values.push(additionalFields.baseline_ready);
1246:     }

1251:     if (additionalFields.error_code !== undefined) {

1255:     if (additionalFields.error_message !== undefined) {
1256:       updates.push('error_message = ?');

1259:   }
1260: 
1261:   values.push(id);
1262: 
1263:   let whereClause = 'id = ?';

1406: 
1407:   if (existingIdempotency && existingIdempotency.expires_at > now) {
1408:     if (existingIdempotency.request_payload_sha256 !== input.requestPayloadSha256) {
1409:       throw new WorkspaceIdempotencyConflictError(input.idempotencyKey, input.type);

1412:     const existingOperation = await getWorkspaceOperation(db, input.workspaceId, existingIdempotency.operation_id);
1413:     if (!existingOperation) {

1415:     }
1416:     return { operation: existingOperation, reused: true };

1418: 
1419:   if (existingIdempotency && existingIdempotency.expires_at <= now) {
1420:     await db
1421:       .prepare(
1422:         `DELETE FROM workspace_operation_idempotency
1423:          WHERE workspace_id = ? AND operation_type = ? AND idempotency_key = ?`
1424:       )

1429:   const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
1430:   const operationId = input.id;
1431: 
1432:   const operationRecord = await db
1433:     .prepare(
1434:       `INSERT INTO workspace_operations (
1435:          id,
1436:          workspace_id,
1437:          type,
1438:          status,
1439:          actor_id,

1442:          request_payload_sha256,
1443:          idempotency_key,
1444:          warnings_json
1445:        )
1446:        VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'queued', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, '[]')
1447:        RETURNING *`
1448:     )
1449:     .bind(
1450:       operationId,
1451:       input.workspaceId,
1452:       input.type,
1453:       input.actorId ?? null,
1454:       JSON.stringify(input.authPrincipal ?? {}),
1455:       JSON.stringify(input.requestPayload ?? {}),
1456:       input.requestPayloadSha256,
1457:       input.idempotencyKey
1458:     )

1460: 
1461:   if (!operationRecord) {
1462:     throw new Error('Failed to create workspace operation');
1463:   }
1464: 
1465:   try {
1466:     await db
1467:       .prepare(
1468:         `INSERT INTO workspace_operation_idempotency (
1469:            id,
1470:            workspace_id,
1471:            operation_type,
1472:            idempotency_key,
1473:            operation_id,
1474:            request_payload_sha256,
1475:            expires_at
1476:          )
1477:          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`
1478:       )
1479:       .bind(
1480:         generatePrefixedId('wopk'),
1481:         input.workspaceId,
1482:         input.type,
1483:         input.idempotencyKey,
1484:         operationId,

1487:       )

1493:     }
1494: 
1495:     const concurrentIdempotency = await db
1496:       .prepare(
1497:         `SELECT operation_id, request_payload_sha256, expires_at
1498:          FROM workspace_operation_idempotency
1499:          WHERE workspace_id = ? AND operation_type = ? AND idempotency_key = ?
1500:          LIMIT 1`
1501:       )
1502:       .bind(input.workspaceId, input.type, input.idempotencyKey)
1503:       .first<{ operation_id: string; request_payload_sha256: string; expires_at: string }>();
1504: 
1505:     if (!concurrentIdempotency || concurrentIdempotency.expires_at <= now) {

1508:     }
1509:     if (concurrentIdempotency.request_payload_sha256 !== input.requestPayloadSha256) {

1512:     }

1514:     const existingOperation = await getWorkspaceOperation(db, input.workspaceId, concurrentIdempotency.operation_id);
1515:     if (!existingOperation) {
1516:       await db.prepare('DELETE FROM workspace_operations WHERE id = ?').bind(operationId).run();
1517:       throw new Error(`Idempotency record references missing operation ${concurrentIdempotency.operation_id}`);

1519:     await db.prepare('DELETE FROM workspace_operations WHERE id = ?').bind(operationId).run();

1589:   if (options?.result !== undefined) {

1593:   if (options?.warnings !== undefined) {
1594:     updates.push('warnings_json = ?');
1595:     values.push(JSON.stringify(options.warnings));
1596:   }
1597:   if (options?.errorCode !== undefined) {
1598:     updates.push('error_code = ?');
1599:     values.push(options.errorCode);
1600:   }
1601:   if (options?.errorClass !== undefined) {
1602:     updates.push('error_class = ?');
1603:     values.push(options.errorClass);
1604:   }
1605:   if (options?.errorMessage !== undefined) {
1606:     updates.push('error_message = ?');
1607:     values.push(options.errorMessage);

1609:   if (options?.errorDetails !== undefined) {
1610:     updates.push('error_details_json = ?');
1611:     values.push(JSON.stringify(options.errorDetails));

1613:   if (status === 'running') {
1614:     updates.push('started_at = COALESCE(started_at, ?)');

1617:   if (status === 'succeeded' || status === 'failed') {

1620:     updates.push('duration_ms = CASE WHEN started_at IS NULL OR COALESCE(finished_at, ?) IS NULL THEN NULL ELSE CAST((julianday(COALESCE(finished_at, ?)) - julianday(started_at)) * 86400000 AS INTEGER) END');

1624: 
1625:   values.push(operationId);
1626:

1734:         await replaceReviewFindings(env.DB, reviewId, []);

1741:         await appendReviewEvent(env.DB, {

3241: export async function getReviewCochangeCacheBatch(
3242:   db: D1Database,
3243:   input: {
3244:     repo: string;
3245:     filePaths: string[];
3246:   }
3247: ): Promise<Array<{ filePath: string; cochange: Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>; lastUpdated: string; lookbackSessions: number }>> {
3248:   const READ_CHUNK_SIZE = 20;
3249:   const isBindLimitError = (error: unknown): boolean => {
3250:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
3251:     return /too many sql variables|sqlite_error/i.test(message);
3252:   };
3253:   const uniquePaths = Array.from(new Set(input.filePaths.map((value) => value.trim()).filter(Boolean)));
3254:   if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
3255:     return [];
3256:   }
3257: 
3258:   const output: Array<{ filePath: string; cochange: Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }>; lastUpdated: string; lookbackSessions: number }> = [];
3259: 
3260:   const queryChunk = async (chunk: string[]): Promise<Array<{ file_path: string; cochange_json: string; last_updated: string; lookback_sessions: number }>> => {
3261:     const placeholders = chunk.map(() => '?').join(', ');
3262:     try {
3263:       const records = await db
3264:         .prepare(
3265:           `SELECT file_path, cochange_json, last_updated, lookback_sessions
3266:            FROM review_cochange_cache
3267:            WHERE repo = ? AND file_path IN (${placeholders})`
3268:         )
3269:         .bind(input.repo, ...chunk)
3270:         .all<{ file_path: string; cochange_json: string; last_updated: string; lookback_sessions: number }>();
3271:       return Array.isArray(records.results) ? records.results : [];
3272:     } catch (error) {
3273:       if (chunk.length > 1 && isBindLimitError(error)) {
3274:         const midpoint = Math.ceil(chunk.length / 2);
3275:         const left = await queryChunk(chunk.slice(0, midpoint));
3276:         const right = await queryChunk(chunk.slice(midpoint));
3277:         return [...left, ...right];
3278:       }
3279:       throw error;
3280:     }
3281:   };
3282: 
3283:   for (let index = 0; index < uniquePaths.length; index += READ_CHUNK_SIZE) {
3284:     const chunk = uniquePaths.slice(index, index + READ_CHUNK_SIZE);
3285:     const rows = await queryChunk(chunk);
3286:     output.push(
3287:       ...rows.map((record) => {
3288:         let cochange: Array<{ path: string; frequency: number; sessionIds: string[] }> = [];
3289:         try {
3290:           const parsed = JSON.parse(record.cochange_json) as unknown;

3293:               const entry = item as Record<string, unknown>;
3294:               return typeof entry.path === 'string' && typeof entry.frequency === 'number' && Array.isArray(entry.sessionIds);
3295:             });
3296:           }
3297:         } catch {
3298:           cochange = [];
3299:         }
3300: 
3301:         return {
3302:           filePath: record.file_path,
3303:           cochange,
3304:           lastUpdated: record.last_updated,
3305:           lookbackSessions: Number(record.lookback_sessions),

state.title: packages/report-ui/src/lib/review.test.ts
review_context_assembly_

parts.text:
- **Medium – stale-run recovery in read API bypasses retry scheduling**
  - `packages/worker/src/api/reviews.ts:63` always forces stale `running` reviews to `failed` (`review_execution_timeout`), while the runner path is designed to requeue stale runs when retry budget remains (`packages/worker/src/lib/review-runner.ts:1711`).
  - Scenario: a review is stale on attempt 1 (or 2), user/UI calls `GET /api/reviews/:id`, and this API path marks it terminally failed instead of `retry_scheduled`; subsequent queue processing won’t retry because status is no longer `queued`.
  - Action: align API stale-recovery behavior with runner behavior (queue retry when `attemptCount <= REVIEW_MAX_RETRIES`, otherwise fail).

- **Low – context failure guidance branch is effectively unreachable**
  - `packages/report-ui/src/lib/review.ts:443` checks `code.startsWith('review_context_assembly_')`, but worker error codes emitted for context failures are `review_context_*` (e.g. `review_context_github_token_missing`, `review_context_cache_error`).
  - Scenario: a context failure occurs; UI falls back to generic “Review execution failed” guidance instead of the intended context-specific remediation.
  - Action: match on actual emitted prefix(es), e.g. `review_context_`, or an explicit allowlist.

- **Low – polling refresh forces full loading state every cycle**
  - `packages/report-ui/src/components/ReportPage.tsx:171` sets `state='loading'` on every refresh (`refreshCycle`), so queued/running pages repeatedly flip to loading during polling.
  - Scenario: long-running review; every 3s refresh can cause visible flicker and temporarily hide status/details.
  - Action: keep prior loaded UI during background refresh (separate `isRefreshing` flag), and only use full loading for initial fetch.
